整套首页
unit_23 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_23

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 510 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
Unquestionably, the particular forms that folly and cruelty take in Jane Austen's novels are (i)__________ the character's social milieu, which was also Austen's own; but to realize that one's society motivates people in unfortunate ways is not necessarily to (ii)__________ it, for the alternatives, though different, might be no more (iii)__________.Unquestionably, the particular forms that folly and cruelty take in Jane Austen's novels are (i)__________ the character's social milieu, which was also Austen's own; but to realize that one's society motivates people in unfortunate ways is not necessarily to (ii)__________ it, for the alternatives, though different, might be no more (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　shaped by; condemn; salutary
milieunovelrationalize
milieu: standard unit headword | novel: standard unit headword | rationalize: standard unit headword
#2 SE
One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely __________ fossils throughout his career.One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely __________ fossils throughout his career.
答案：A, F　counterfeit; spurious
plausiblespuriousrefractory
plausible: standard unit headword | spurious: standard unit headword | refractory: standard unit headword
#3 TC SINGLE
Barring the discovery of new letters, hidden diaries, or the like, fresh information about eminent people is hard to find because their lives have been so intensely __________.Barring the discovery of new letters, hidden diaries, or the like, fresh information about eminent people is hard to find because their lives have been so intensely __________.
答案：B　scrutinized
barbarring
bar: standard unit headword | barring: standard unit headword
#4 TC DOUBLE
The virtual absence of cougars from late prehistoric faunas in the North American Great Basin (i) __________ a general scarcity of carnivores from these sites: bobcats, coyotes, and badgers are routinely found, and even such historically (ii) __________ carnivores as bears and wolves are found as well.The virtual absence of cougars from late prehistoric faunas in the North American Great Basin (i) __________ a general scarcity of carnivores from these sites: bobcats, coyotes, and badgers are routinely found, and even such historically (ii) __________ carnivores as bears and wolves are found as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　does not reflect; rare
scarcitycarnivore
scarcity: standard unit headword | carnivore: standard unit headword
#5 TC TRIPLE
A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)__________. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)__________ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)__________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)__________. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)__________ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)__________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough
roildiminish
roil: standard unit headword | diminish: standard unit headword
#6 TC SINGLE
The paleontologist examined the problem afresh, believing that the accepted classification __________ the essential continuity of the specimens by making specious distinctions among them.The paleontologist examined the problem afresh, believing that the accepted classification __________ the essential continuity of the specimens by making specious distinctions among them.
答案：B　belied
belieafresh
belie: standard unit headword | afresh: standard unit headword

The major __________ of such popular history is that it betrays no interests in making intellectual contributions to our understanding of an issue.The major __________ of such popular history is that it betrays no interests in making intellectual contributions to our understanding of an issue.
答案：B, D　shortcoming; fault
issuebetray
issue: standard unit headword | betray: standard unit headword

While acknowledging behaviors the Prime Minister took in order to remain in office were (i)__________, some politicians nevertheless believed that this small amount of (ii)__________ was justified to keep a reforming government in office.While acknowledging behaviors the Prime Minister took in order to remain in office were (i)__________, some politicians nevertheless believed that this small amount of (ii)__________ was justified to keep a reforming government in office.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　unethical; skullduggery
justifyquixotic
justify: standard unit headword | quixotic: standard unit headword
#9 TC TRIPLE
The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes
energizerationale
energize: standard unit headword | rationale: standard unit headword
#10 SE
The town’s air was consistently __________: depending on the breeze, one might be greeted with the sour effluvia of twenty breweries, choking fumes from the coal factory, or brackish smells from the nearby river.The town’s air was consistently __________: depending on the breeze, one might be greeted with the sour effluvia of twenty breweries, choking fumes from the coal factory, or brackish smells from the nearby river.
答案：A, E　malodorous; noisome

toxic: standard unit headword | greet: standard unit headword

答案：C　capricious

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　in overall equilibrium; fluctuations

However, evidence of significant contact between the ancient Mediterranean and sub-Saharan Africa has been (ii) __________, rendering these exogenous explanations (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　recipients of; meager; tenuous

suggestive: standard unit headword | adversary: standard unit headword

答案：E, F　lugubrious; gloomy

答案：B　wherewithal

Blank 3

This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)__________ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　an impediment to; hasty; stagnation

答案：A, F　burdensome; onerous

burdensome: standard unit headword | litigation: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　a difficult; an enigmatic
qualitywhimsical
quality: standard unit headword | whimsical: standard unit headword

Moreover, high oil prices had themselves started to act as a short-term (ii)__________ the global economy, thus further dampening demand.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　slowed; drag on
slacken

Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)__________ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)__________ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)__________ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　naturalness; anthropogenic
virgin
virgin: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　noticeable; conspicuous

Blank 3

答案：D　matched

It has been documented more than once that a group of whales will (ii)__________ its own tunes and adopt the new sounds of an unfamiliar group.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　tastes; abandon
document

People who are reluctant to oppose a court nominee straightforwardly on ideological grounds often search for any sort of peccadillo to serve as a __________ their opposition.People who are reluctant to oppose a court nominee straightforwardly on ideological grounds often search for any sort of peccadillo to serve as a __________ their opposition.
答案：E　pretext for

The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)__________. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)__________. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility
quotidian
quotidian: standard unit headword

In years prior to the Civil War, Philadelphia’s African American press encouraged readers to be vaccinated against the smallpox. This journalistic campaign was initially (i)__________, appealing to readers’ sense of communal duty, and became even more (ii)__________ once the war started, as smallpox outbreaks began to occur on Philadelphia’s city skirts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　hortatory; urgent
skirt

Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　energy; flush with; tedium

#53 SE
The dictator’s gleaming military uniform and imperial paraphernalia sharply contrast with the __________ fashion favored by most other contemporary political leaders.The dictator’s gleaming military uniform and imperial paraphernalia sharply contrast with the __________ fashion favored by most other contemporary political leaders.
答案：A, B　unostentatious; modest
imperial
imperial: standard unit headword
#54 TC DOUBLE
Recently the novelist has (i) __________ the radically experimental forms with which he made his reputation in favor of more (ii) __________ narratives, fencing in and turning an imagination meant to run wild.Recently the novelist has (i) __________ the radically experimental forms with which he made his reputation in favor of more (ii) __________ narratives, fencing in and turning an imagination meant to run wild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　forsaken; conventional
fence
fence: standard unit headword
#55 TC TRIPLE
Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　reverence for; exemplary; irreconcilable
accomplished
accomplished: standard unit headword
#56 SE
Three of the nation's largest airlines could be operating under bankruptcy protection in coming weeks, analysts say, the latest sign of the industry's __________ as it lurches through a historic transformation.Three of the nation's largest airlines could be operating under bankruptcy protection in coming weeks, analysts say, the latest sign of the industry's __________ as it lurches through a historic transformation.
答案：A, E　upheaval; convulsion
convulsion
convulsion: standard unit headword
#57 TC SINGLE
Among the Meakambut people of Papua New Guinea, legends are associated with specific caves in the Sepik region, and the legends are __________: only cave owner can share its secrets.Among the Meakambut people of Papua New Guinea, legends are associated with specific caves in the Sepik region, and the legends are __________: only cave owner can share its secrets.
答案：A　proprietary
didactic
didactic: standard unit headword
#58 TC DOUBLE
It is an old vice of criticism--and lately of scholarship--to assume that the meaning of artworks is (i) __________ by their creators and is then simply "there," waiting only to be decoded by a specially gifted interpreter. That assumption can lead to gross errors, as with the critical interpretations of Theodor Adorno, which are (ii) __________ by their dependence on the assumption.It is an old vice of criticism--and lately of scholarship--to assume that the meaning of artworks is (i) __________ by their creators and is then simply "there," waiting only to be decoded by a specially gifted interpreter. That assumption can lead to gross errors, as with the critical interpretations of Theodor Adorno, which are (ii) __________ by their dependence on the assumption.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　fully vested in them; vitiated
decode
decode: standard unit headword
#59 TC TRIPLE
Biologists have little (i)__________ drawing the link between the success of humanity and human (ii)__________. Indeed, many biologists claim that this attribute, the ability to (iii)__________, or, to put it more sharply, to make individuals subordinate their self-interest to the needs of the group, lies at the root of human achievement.Biologists have little (i)__________ drawing the link between the success of humanity and human (ii)__________. Indeed, many biologists claim that this attribute, the ability to (iii)__________, or, to put it more sharply, to make individuals subordinate their self-interest to the needs of the group, lies at the root of human achievement.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　compunction about; sociability; cooperate
compunctionjustification
compunction: standard unit headword | justification: standard unit headword
#60 TC SINGLE
Though humanitarian emergencies are frequent features of television news, such exposure seldom __________ the public, which rather seems resigned to a sense of impotency.Though humanitarian emergencies are frequent features of television news, such exposure seldom __________ the public, which rather seems resigned to a sense of impotency.
答案：D　galvanizes
exasperate
exasperate: standard unit headword
#61 SE
Although evolutionary psychologists do not seem quite as imperialist in their intellectual ambitions as their sociobiologist forebears of the 1970s, they tend, in some critics’ view, to be no less __________ in their claims.Although evolutionary psychologists do not seem quite as imperialist in their intellectual ambitions as their sociobiologist forebears of the 1970s, they tend, in some critics’ view, to be no less __________ in their claims.
答案：B, D　arrogant; hubristic
imperialist

The 1938 "War of the Worlds" fiasco, in which millions of people supposedly believed that a fictional radio program about a Martian invasion was true, is held up as an example of the (i) __________ of the American public. While Americans are portrayed as (ii) __________ in this version of the story, research reveals that most listeners knew the program was fictional.The 1938 "War of the Worlds" fiasco, in which millions of people supposedly believed that a fictional radio program about a Martian invasion was true, is held up as an example of the (i) __________ of the American public. While Americans are portrayed as (ii) __________ in this version of the story, research reveals that most listeners knew the program was fictional.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　credulity; gullible
gullible
gullible: standard unit headword
#63 TC TRIPLE
The characters in this comic strip fret about the (i)__________ of their “little counterculture lives”, especially when terrible things are happening in the world. But the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)__________ in ways that do not seem (iii)__________ at all. Real things happen here – births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil union – and they matter.The characters in this comic strip fret about the (i)__________ of their “little counterculture lives”, especially when terrible things are happening in the world. But the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)__________ in ways that do not seem (iii)__________ at all. Real things happen here – births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil union – and they matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　triviality; resonate; inconsequential
stagnate
stagnate: standard unit headword

答案：E　sanguine
magisterial

When deciding from whom to learn, children are surprisingly __________: by early preschool, they rely on multiple cues when determining informant credibility, such as prior accuracy in a particular domain, benevolence, and social group status.When deciding from whom to learn, children are surprisingly __________: by early preschool, they rely on multiple cues when determining informant credibility, such as prior accuracy in a particular domain, benevolence, and social group status.
答案：C, F　selective; discerning

Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder
plausibleunerring

The journalist was someone whose habitual distrust of authority struck a few people as (i)__________ but who had enough talent and charm that most found the trait to be (ii)__________, with the result that it did not become a personal or professional liability.The journalist was someone whose habitual distrust of authority struck a few people as (i)__________ but who had enough talent and charm that most found the trait to be (ii)__________, with the result that it did not become a personal or professional liability.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　pathological; pardonable

答案：C　surreptitious

答案：A, E　a mainstay of; a foundation of

Blank 2
答案：A, E　intransigent; uncompromising

答案：A　sophist

Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of __________ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of __________ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.
答案：D, F　proscriptive; exacting

To read Joanna Scott is to admire the work of a (i) __________. From sentence to story, she narrates with great skill and (ii) __________, so that the reader soon relaxes in the assurance that a hint or a brushstroke delivered in chapter 1 will be (iii) __________ before the novel comes to an end.To read Joanna Scott is to admire the work of a (i) __________. From sentence to story, she narrates with great skill and (ii) __________, so that the reader soon relaxes in the assurance that a hint or a brushstroke delivered in chapter 1 will be (iii) __________ before the novel comes to an end.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　master; deliberation; given import

By deliberately dripping paint on their canvases, expressionists affirmed that paint is not __________ entity, something merely to be moved where the artist pleases, but rather a material possessing a fluid energy that the painter attempts to control.By deliberately dripping paint on their canvases, expressionists affirmed that paint is not __________ entity, something merely to be moved where the artist pleases, but rather a material possessing a fluid energy that the painter attempts to control.
答案：D　a passive

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：B　interrogated

答案：B, D　precarious; perilous

答案：A, D　preachy; sanctimonious

However, the fact that science--even "pure" science--can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be: scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science--even "pure" science--can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. Blank 2
Blank 3

His premiership, seemingly cast-iron a year ago, is now so vulnerable that even a good day at the office does no more than buy him a few weeks of __________ from rebels within his own party.His premiership, seemingly cast-iron a year ago, is now so vulnerable that even a good day at the office does no more than buy him a few weeks of __________ from rebels within his own party.
答案：C, D　respite; relief

答案：C, E　unabated; undiminished

Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news—tales of how things went wrong—but we have only the most __________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news—tales of how things went wrong—but we have only the most __________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：D, F　cursory; perfunctory

Others have replied to this with variation on the theme of how we still hold footraces despite cars going much faster, but this analogy is (ii) __________ since cars do not help humans run faster while chess computers undoubtedly have an effect on the (iii) __________ of human chess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　losing interest in; spurious; quality

Blank 2

Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
issuelitigation
issue: standard unit headword | litigation: standard unit headword

答案：B　abstemiousness

Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)__________ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)__________ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)__________ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)__________ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)__________ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)__________ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　attend disproportionately to; responsiveness to; mask
plausibleissue

After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii) __________ rarely precedes creative glory. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii) __________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzy about comics today is (i) __________. After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii) __________ rarely precedes creative glory. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii) __________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　understandable; arousal; breakthrough

Blank 2
Blank 3

Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　consistent; espoused; insufficient

#104 TC DOUBLE
Memory-prompt technology such as online birthday reminders does more than enhance our recall abilities; it induces us to (i)__________ ever more behaviors to automated process. Witness the (ii)__________ a program that allows us to create computer greeting cards for the entire year in one sitting.Memory-prompt technology such as online birthday reminders does more than enhance our recall abilities; it induces us to (i)__________ ever more behaviors to automated process. Witness the (ii)__________ a program that allows us to create computer greeting cards for the entire year in one sitting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　delegate; popularity of
greet
greet: standard unit headword
#105 TC TRIPLE
The nature of our recollections is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine
quality
quality: standard unit headword
#106 TC SINGLE
Dr. Jack Kevorkian became a subject of controversy in the 1990s by assisting terminally ill people to commit suicide in contravention of Michigan statutes' __________ such actions.Dr. Jack Kevorkian became a subject of controversy in the 1990s by assisting terminally ill people to commit suicide in contravention of Michigan statutes' __________ such actions.
答案：B　proscribing
proscribejustify
proscribe: standard unit headword | justify: standard unit headword
#107 TC DOUBLE
Far from (i)__________ the actions taken by the newspaper’s executives, Willem praised the executives’ resistance to corruption – yet he doubted that their policies were practical enough to warrant (ii)__________ by other papers.Far from (i)__________ the actions taken by the newspaper’s executives, Willem praised the executives’ resistance to corruption – yet he doubted that their policies were practical enough to warrant (ii)__________ by other papers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　impugning; emulation
warrant
warrant: standard unit headword
#108 TC TRIPLE
It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy
plausible
plausible: standard unit headword
#109 TC TRIPLE
Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)__________ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)__________. Thus today in the (iii)__________ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)__________ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)__________. Thus today in the (iii)__________ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　supplanted; an act of private charity; absence
supplant
supplant: standard unit headword
#110 TC SINGLE
What once seemed a quixotic vision—the “Subway to the Sea,” connecting Union Station in downtown Los Angeles to the Pacific Ocean in Santa Monica—no longer seems quite so __________.What once seemed a quixotic vision—the “Subway to the Sea,” connecting Union Station in downtown Los Angeles to the Pacific Ocean in Santa Monica—no longer seems quite so __________.
答案：A　impracticable
quixotic
quixotic: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE
“Argument” may be an overly (i)__________ word to apply to the gossamer contrivance that is A Summer of Hummingbirds. In what seems a self-conscious (ii)__________ of its mascot, the book flits from one subjects or moment in history to another, following the various whims of its author.“Argument” may be an overly (i)__________ word to apply to the gossamer contrivance that is A Summer of Hummingbirds. In what seems a self-conscious (ii)__________ of its mascot, the book flits from one subjects or moment in history to another, following the various whims of its author.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　robust; emulation

答案：D, E　belie; mask
belie
belie: standard unit headword
#113 TC SINGLE
By pointing out the self-serving nature of the governor’s motives for supporting the new health care policy, the columnist implied that the governor’s idealistic-sounding explanation of her position on the issue was almost certainly __________.By pointing out the self-serving nature of the governor’s motives for supporting the new health care policy, the columnist implied that the governor’s idealistic-sounding explanation of her position on the issue was almost certainly __________.
答案：D　disingenuous
issue

#114 TC TRIPLE
If you wish to make a novel (i)__________ a field of study, you must master what scholars have already said on the subject but at the same time keep in mind the (ii)__________ received opinion, which can (iii)__________ a nascent idea before it can develop.If you wish to make a novel (i)__________ a field of study, you must master what scholars have already said on the subject but at the same time keep in mind the (ii)__________ received opinion, which can (iii)__________ a nascent idea before it can develop.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　contribution to; oppressive influence of; quash

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　exploited; downplay the legitimacy

In Inuit culture, elaborate carving has often been used to enhance __________ objects such as harpoon heads and other tools.In Inuit culture, elaborate carving has often been used to enhance __________ objects such as harpoon heads and other tools.
答案：A, B　utilitarian; functional
functional
functional: standard unit headword

答案：E　concocting

#118 TC TRIPLE
China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)__________; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)__________. This human pressure has (iii)__________ the South China tiger.China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)__________; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)__________. This human pressure has (iii)__________ the South China tiger.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　implemented; can grow unchecked; doomed

The guilty-pleasure of the play lies in its invitation for us to identify with its characters--attractive, articulate young women and young men (i) __________ behavior that, from the outside, may look more than a little (ii) __________: though the actions are initially repugnant, we cannot help but share the point of view of the characters committing them.The guilty-pleasure of the play lies in its invitation for us to identify with its characters--attractive, articulate young women and young men (i) __________ behavior that, from the outside, may look more than a little (ii) __________: though the actions are initially repugnant, we cannot help but share the point of view of the characters committing them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　rationalize; monstrous

答案：E　credibility

#122 TC TRIPLE

The (ii)__________ of good storm-intensity forecasts has huge economic costs, but filling that need won’t automatically generate big profits because the economic benefits are (iii)__________.His idea to make money by building a drone that could fly from the West Coast to Hawaii while continuously sending back low-altitude weather data is a characteristically (i)__________ project. The (ii)__________ of good storm-intensity forecasts has huge economic costs, but filling that need won’t automatically generate big profits because the economic benefits are (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　quixotic; dearth; so widely diffused

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　untrammeled; implausibility

答案：A　significance

With a single track bisecting the state and a handful of spokes to the east and south, Alaska is notable for its (iii)__________ of transportation options.

答案：D　linked

Blank 3

答案：D　astringent

#134 TC TRIPLE
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized

Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earned scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i)__________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudosciences, much of the writing has a (ii)__________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earned scolding tone common to many such endeavors.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　inadequacies; debunking

While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale’s creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy’s nonfiction.Leo Tolstoy wrote many works of nonfiction and professed (i)__________ these explorations of ethics and religion compared with his novels and short stories. Handi Murdd is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale’s creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy’s nonfiction.
Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：A, D, G　a preference for; suppress; didacticism

Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)__________ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)__________ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering

It allows people to avoid embarrassing or maligning an adversary, and it serves as a form of irony that draw attention to a problem that can scarcely be exaggerated.

Blank 2

答案：D　insurmountable

#149 SE
British critics covering African American musicians performing in London in the 1910s had little idea how to distinguish what was authentic African American music from what was __________, but they knew such a distinction existed.British critics covering African American musicians performing in London in the 1910s had little idea how to distinguish what was authentic African American music from what was __________, but they knew such a distinction existed.
答案：D, F　spurious; specious

It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain

Blank 2

答案：A, D　plausible; conceivable

答案：A　an etiolated

答案：A, E　promotes; constraints

Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)__________; nevertheless, the country's vast size and the isolation of many of its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)__________. This human pressure has (iii)__________ the South China tiger.China's rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People's Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)__________; nevertheless, the country's vast size and the isolation of many of its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)__________. This human pressure has (iii)__________ the South China tiger.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　implemented; can grow unchecked; doomed
carnivore
carnivore: standard unit headword
#156 SE
Those who read Empson’s correspondence for the first time may be disappointed that so much of it is professional rather than personal, but the distinction in this case is __________: every letter bears the full stamp of Empson’s personality.Those who read Empson’s correspondence for the first time may be disappointed that so much of it is professional rather than personal, but the distinction in this case is __________: every letter bears the full stamp of Empson’s personality.
答案：B, D　artificial; spurious
spurious
spurious: standard unit headword
#157 TC SINGLE
The fiftieth birthday of this much-admired musician has, predictably, given rise to various __________, including two flattering television documentaries about her life.The fiftieth birthday of this much-admired musician has, predictably, given rise to various __________, including two flattering television documentaries about her life.
答案：A　encomiums
documentary
documentary: standard unit headword
#158 TC DOUBLE
Readers may initially be irked by the book's apparent (i) __________ but, once immersed in the author's prose, they may come to regard the work's (ii) __________ as an asset.Readers may initially be irked by the book's apparent (i) __________ but, once immersed in the author's prose, they may come to regard the work's (ii) __________ as an asset.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　aimlessness; discursions
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword
#159 TC TRIPLE
Parker's model of human affairs reflects (i) __________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii) __________ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii) __________ theirs.Parker's model of human affairs reflects (i) __________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii) __________ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii) __________ theirs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　a sanguine; pessimistic; diverge markedly
diverge
diverge: standard unit headword

The __________ directed toward the editor struck many observers as excessive, since the quality of the special sections, she oversaw, while far from perfect, was unmatched by any other contemporary newspaper.The __________ directed toward the editor struck many observers as excessive, since the quality of the special sections, she oversaw, while far from perfect, was unmatched by any other contemporary newspaper.
答案：C　contumely
quality
quality: standard unit headword

Although its director __________ that the movie uses a documentary approach in portraying the famous sit-down strike, in practice its characters are heavily fictionalized and fall into familiar Hollywood types.Although its director __________ that the movie uses a documentary approach in portraying the famous sit-down strike, in practice its characters are heavily fictionalized and fall into familiar Hollywood types.
答案：A, F　asserts; maintains
documentary
documentary: standard unit headword
#162 TC TRIPLE
The limitations of human attention cause us to miss much of what goes on around us. The real problem here is that we are often (i)__________ these limitations: we think that we see the world as it really is, but our ostensibly reliable visual experience (ii)__________ striking mental (iii)__________.The limitations of human attention cause us to miss much of what goes on around us. The real problem here is that we are often (i)__________ these limitations: we think that we see the world as it really is, but our ostensibly reliable visual experience (ii)__________ striking mental (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　unaware of; belies; lapses
belie
belie: standard unit headword
#163 TC DOUBLE
The description of Green's scholarship as (i)__________ is grossly misleading: while her research on interstellar particles is not especially novel, the conclusions she draws from her data are (ii)__________.The description of Green's scholarship as (i)__________ is grossly misleading: while her research on interstellar particles is not especially novel, the conclusions she draws from her data are (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　derivative; remarkably pioneering
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#164 TC SINGLE
Though Blandford's manifesto envisions the victory of his political philosophy as __________, Dreyer denies that the manifesto is a deterministic document.Though Blandford's manifesto envisions the victory of his political philosophy as __________, Dreyer denies that the manifesto is a deterministic document.
答案：E　inevitable
document
document: standard unit headword
#165 SE
Although one can adduce myriad of examples of ecosystem disruption by nonindigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survived in fact appear to have quite __________ effects on the ecosystem they have invaded.Although one can adduce myriad of examples of ecosystem disruption by nonindigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survived in fact appear to have quite __________ effects on the ecosystem they have invaded.
答案：C, E　minimal; trifling
myriad
myriad: standard unit headword
#166 TC TRIPLE
When pulsars were first discovered, some of the astrophysicists involved briefly (i) __________ these types of stars might be (ii) __________ extraterrestrial intelligence. Specifically, the pulsar's periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii) __________ rather than--as it turned out to be--the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.When pulsars were first discovered, some of the astrophysicists involved briefly (i) __________ these types of stars might be (ii) __________ extraterrestrial intelligence. Specifically, the pulsar's periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii) __________ rather than--as it turned out to be--the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　thought that; suggestive of; a constructed code
suggestive
suggestive: standard unit headword

It would be imprecise to characterize her scholarship as (i) __________: though her etymological discussion is necessarily esoteric, there is nothing (ii) __________ about the conclusions she derives from it.It would be imprecise to characterize her scholarship as (i) __________: though her etymological discussion is necessarily esoteric, there is nothing (ii) __________ about the conclusions she derives from it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　arcane; obscure
characterize

答案：A　perplexing
characterize
characterize: standard unit headword

答案：B, C　original; novel

答案：A　crude

Moreover, high oil prices had themselves started to act as a short-term (ii) __________ the global economy, thus further dampening demand.

The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：C　formal

Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earnest scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i) __________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudoscience, much of the writing has a (ii) __________ quality to it. Braque and Picasso created Cubism together, but the mercurial Picasso is regarded as the star of the movement while Braque, because he was so much more (ii) __________, has been relatively (iii) __________ by history.The quiet, careful member of a collaborative, creative pair may naturally be (i) __________ by posterity in comparison with the brash, dramatic one. 答案：D　tautological

答案：C, F　primitive; rudimentary

Rhetorical styles might vary, but style itself was never a matter of (ii) __________. And "style" was not just a well-turned sentence: poor expression, it was believed, (iii) __________ poor thought.Our contemporary preference for natural expression over artifice is (i) __________ the traditional Western view that the skill with which you expressed a position corresponded closely to the credibility of your argument. Recent scholarship has questioned the (i) __________ of tropical forests around the world. Archaeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa's savanna forest transition zone are (ii) __________ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i) __________ of tropical forests around the world. Archaeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa's savanna forest transition zone are (ii) __________ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　naturalness; anthropogenic

答案：A, D　quiescence; calm

It was from the third decade of the eighteenth century that the two terms began to (ii)__________. Newman and Principe suggest that a third term, "chymistry," should be used to denote the transitional early modern phase when "alchemy" and "chemistry" were becoming (iii)__________.William Newman and Lawrence Principe point out that prior to the seventeenth century the terms "alchemy" and "chemistry" were largely (i)__________ in European usage. It was from the third decade of the eighteenth century that the two terms began to (ii)__________. Newman and Principe suggest that a third term, "chymistry," should be used to denote the transitional early modern phase when "alchemy" and "chemistry" were becoming (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　interchangeable; diverge; steadily less synonymous

答案：B　cacophonous

It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for Me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda's fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for Me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain

Blank 2
答案：C, E　exploited; downplay the legitimacy

答案：B, D　belying; contradicting

答案：A　raze

Blank 2

答案：E, F　heterogeneous; dissimilar

This assumption, however, is (i) __________ the (ii) __________ of "ought" sentences in English: the word is used in too many ways to admit a single definition.Ethical theorists often assume that the verb "ought" means roughly "has an obligation." This assumption, however, is (i) __________ the (ii) __________ of "ought" sentences in English: the word is used in too many ways to admit a single definition.

The fiction writer in him, however, was hard to (ii)__________. Hadji Murad is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale's creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy's nonfiction.Leo Tolstoy wrote many works of nonfiction and professed (i)__________ these explorations of ethics and religion compared with his novels and short stories. The fiction writer in him, however, was hard to (ii)__________. Hadji Murad is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　a preference for; suppress; didacticism

答案：A　jarring

答案：D, F　prevented; precluded

Blank 2

In her novel, by contrast, she was marked by a (ii)__________: she always (iii)__________.What she disliked in the fictions of some of her peers was the excessive (i)__________. In her novel, by contrast, she was marked by a (ii)__________: she always (iii)__________.

答案：A　teem
roil

#209 TC DOUBLE
The company CEO's easygoing manner (i) __________ his (ii) __________ the job: he was in his office by dawn and hard at work till sunset.The company CEO's easygoing manner (i) __________ his (ii) __________ the job: he was in his office by dawn and hard at work till sunset.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　belied; intensity on

belie: standard unit headword
#210 SE
Any notion of justice in the fortunes of artists is __________: works of equal value and quality produce quite different returns or no returns at all.Any notion of justice in the fortunes of artists is __________: works of equal value and quality produce quite different returns or no returns at all.
答案：A, C　baseless; untenable
quality
quality: standard unit headword
#211 TC TRIPLE
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword
#212 SE
The space travels described in science fiction stories were always epic adventures, and, when compared with those travels, current journeys in space are much smaller and the tasks performed are far more __________.The space travels described in science fiction stories were always epic adventures, and, when compared with those travels, current journeys in space are much smaller and the tasks performed are far more __________.
答案：B, D　quotidian; prosaic
quotidian
quotidian: standard unit headword

答案：B　unencumbered

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　disingenuous; pride

Proving the forest-climate link through verifiable and experimental scientific means rather than observation was (ii)__________ for these individuals, a situation that eventually led to the link (iii)__________ justifications for forest conservation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　employed; problematic; dropping out of

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：A　impediment to

答案：C, E　relished; delighted in

Although she (ii) __________ many of the letters that she included and made stylistic changes throughout, often for the worse, she was, by the standards of her time, quite (iii) __________ editor with regard to her respect for the original texts.Anna Barbauld's 1804 edition of Richardson's correspondence has been (i) __________. Although she (ii) __________ many of the letters that she included and made stylistic changes throughout, often for the worse, she was, by the standards of her time, quite (iii) __________ editor with regard to her respect for the original texts.

答案：B　stalwart

答案：C, E　obscure; opaque

Shortly after it opened, its roadway proved to be (ii)__________ under certain wind conditions, so it was stiffened. The additional steel that provided that stiffening naturally added weight to the structure, and this made it (iii)__________ later to add a proposed rail system beneath the roadway.The Golden Gate Bridge has been quite (i)__________ by most aesthetic and functional criteria. However, as a structure it has had some limitations. Shortly after it opened, its roadway proved to be (ii)__________ under certain wind conditions, so it was stiffened. The additional steel that provided that stiffening naturally added weight to the structure, and this made it (iii)__________ later to add a proposed rail system beneath the roadway.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　successful; overly flexible; impossible

Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the precession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy
plausible

答案：A, D　trump; supplant

Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Blank 2

答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

#263 TC TRIPLE
As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relativity have been astonishingly (i) __________ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii) __________ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii) __________, a fact that has disallowed theorists--at least so far--from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relativity have been astonishingly (i) __________ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii) __________ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii) __________, a fact that has disallowed theorists--at least so far--from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　successful; posit; deeply at odds with each other
crack
crack: standard unit headword
#264 TC SINGLE

答案：B　a civic

analytical: standard unit headword
#265 SE

答案：D, E　civility; comity

issue: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：A, D　didactic; amount to

#267 TC TRIPLE
Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work, (i) __________ to propose measures that might (ii) __________ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii) __________, they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work, (i) __________ to propose measures that might (ii) __________ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii) __________, they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　forgoing opportunities; ameliorate; reticence
document
document: standard unit headword

答案：B　stalwart

whimsical: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　belied; contradicted

belie: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：A, F　disinclination; callow

Researchers claim that early mammals evolved a multistep process to synthesize vitamin C in their own bodies but that tens of millions of years ago some primates became so (i)__________ eating fruits rich in this vitamin that making their own became (ii)__________. Their physiology (iii)__________ their behavior. Consequently, their descendants, today's anthropoid primates, cannot make vitamin C.Researchers claim that early mammals evolved a multistep process to synthesize vitamin C in their own bodies but that tens of millions of years ago some primates became so (i)__________ eating fruits rich in this vitamin that making their own became (ii)__________. Their physiology (iii)__________ their behavior. Consequently, their descendants, today's anthropoid primates, cannot make vitamin C.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　accustomed to; an unnecessary cost; adapted to

答案：C　require

Blank 2

#275 TC TRIPLE
Elephants are known to use a wide variety of calls, many of them infrasonic (below the level of human hearing), in coordinating group behavior. The infrasonic calls usually have a component that is (i)__________, but these higher frequency aspects of the calls (ii)__________ very quickly with distance, so that in the wild, human observers are often (iii)__________ sudden coordinated movements of large groups of elephants when no signal was apparent to the observer.Elephants are known to use a wide variety of calls, many of them infrasonic (below the level of human hearing), in coordinating group behavior. The infrasonic calls usually have a component that is (i)__________, but these higher frequency aspects of the calls (ii)__________ very quickly with distance, so that in the wild, human observers are often (iii)__________ sudden coordinated movements of large groups of elephants when no signal was apparent to the observer.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　humanly audible; attenuate; astonished by

apparent: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　proscribe; disallow

答案：B　recognized

But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. A badly written book review is worse than a badly written political speech or greeting card; a badly written review is (ii) __________, like a barber with a terrible haircut. If critics cannot write well, how can they authoritatively recognize and promote good writing?To function as (i) __________, literary critics must write well. Blank 1
Blank 2

milieu: standard unit headword
#316 TC DOUBLE
If all stars are fiery gas balls like our own sun, and if the principle that the situation of our own solar system is not (i) __________ is (ii) __________, then one might think that many other stars should be surrounded by a retinue of planets and moons.If all stars are fiery gas balls like our own sun, and if the principle that the situation of our own solar system is not (i) __________ is (ii) __________, then one might think that many other stars should be surrounded by a retinue of planets and moons.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　special; true
fiery
fiery: standard unit headword
#317 SE
This novel is not great literature; there are too many characters, too many subplots, and no clearly defined protagonist, making for __________ story.This novel is not great literature; there are too many characters, too many subplots, and no clearly defined protagonist, making for __________ story.
答案：B, E　a diffuse; an amorphous
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#318 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#319 TC DOUBLE
The apparent promise of clean alternatives to environmentally harmful fossil fuels might actually (i) __________ fossil-fuel industries by framing energy problems not as a matter of excessive consumption but rather as a remediable lack of clean energy production, thus enticing citizens to (ii) __________ the current energy consumption patterns from which fossil-fuel industries profit.The apparent promise of clean alternatives to environmentally harmful fossil fuels might actually (i) __________ fossil-fuel industries by framing energy problems not as a matter of excessive consumption but rather as a remediable lack of clean energy production, thus enticing citizens to (ii) __________ the current energy consumption patterns from which fossil-fuel industries profit.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　benefit; remain complacent about
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword
#320 SE

答案：A, F　routine; grind

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　conviction; unreasonable; feel absolute certainty

答案：A, D　stilted; wooden

The Wildensteins, for instance, were far more successful financially, but they were (iii) __________ beyond measure.As a consequence of his need for (i) __________ and his (ii) __________ self-advertisement, Joseph Duveen's career is better known than those of most of his fellow dealers of museum-quality material. The Wildensteins, for instance, were far more successful financially, but they were (iii) __________ beyond measure.

Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　publicity; indefatigable; secretive

#325 TC DOUBLE
While several relationships fell by the wayside under the pressure of conflicting opinion, none was more affecting to the writer than her relationship with her brother. Her letters document a steady (i) __________ of feeling: intense pride in him giving way to more moderate respect, trust (ii) __________ as feeling of betrayal come to dominate.While several relationships fell by the wayside under the pressure of conflicting opinion, none was more affecting to the writer than her relationship with her brother. Her letters document a steady (i) __________ of feeling: intense pride in him giving way to more moderate respect, trust (ii) __________ as feeling of betrayal come to dominate.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　erosion; evaporating

#326 SE
The author held that since the available evidence used to characterize periods of time many millions to billions of years ago was __________, scientists would likely never obtain completely conclusive answers.The author held that since the available evidence used to characterize periods of time many millions to billions of years ago was __________, scientists would likely never obtain completely conclusive answers.
答案：A, C　sparse; paltry
characterize
characterize: standard unit headword
#327 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#328 TC DOUBLE
Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i) __________ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii) __________, noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i) __________ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii) __________, noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　dearth; demurred

答案：A, C　annalist; chronicler

Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.Because popular culture often portrays lawyers as shady and scientists as eccentric, we might expect cinematic depictions of scientists employed as expert witnesses in court cases to reflect badly on both law and science. Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　suffer reputational losses; tainted; a counterweight to
eccentric
eccentric: standard unit headword
#331 TC DOUBLE
Much scholarship on the early Tana Tradition of coastal East Africa has been characterized by faith in the (i) __________ of that tradition, but detailed analysis of ceramics suggests that there were (ii) __________ the decorative motifs on early Tana Tradition jars, making it difficult to view the various Tana communities as a monolithic whole.Much scholarship on the early Tana Tradition of coastal East Africa has been characterized by faith in the (i) __________ of that tradition, but detailed analysis of ceramics suggests that there were (ii) __________ the decorative motifs on early Tana Tradition jars, making it difficult to view the various Tana communities as a monolithic whole.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　homogeneity; regional differences in
characterize
characterize: standard unit headword

There's __________ throughout the Broadway adaptation of The Color Purple that, while hardly true to the harrowing bleakness of the early chapters of Alice Walker's novel, does bring to mind the enjoyably hokey cinematic lavishness of the film version.There's __________ throughout the Broadway adaptation of The Color Purple that, while hardly true to the harrowing bleakness of the early chapters of Alice Walker's novel, does bring to mind the enjoyably hokey cinematic lavishness of the film version.
答案：B, D　a splendor; a sumptuousness

#333 TC TRIPLE
Ironically, contemporary critics who celebrate the Victorian novel for its intellectual depth describe the (i) __________ effects of newer media - film, television, digital technology - in precisely the same terms that earlier critics had used to (ii) __________ the novel itself: as piecemeal entertainments that (iii) __________ mental effort on the part of the consumer.Ironically, contemporary critics who celebrate the Victorian novel for its intellectual depth describe the (i) __________ effects of newer media - film, television, digital technology - in precisely the same terms that earlier critics had used to (ii) __________ the novel itself: as piecemeal entertainments that (iii) __________ mental effort on the part of the consumer.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　numbing; lambast; obviate
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#334 TC DOUBLE
George Eliot's novels were calculated to inspire sympathy in the reader for characters from a broad range of social classes, but her design was not (i) __________: Eliot hoped her fiction would have a (ii) __________ effect, spurting readers to show charity for those oppressed by the economic inequalities of her age.George Eliot's novels were calculated to inspire sympathy in the reader for characters from a broad range of social classes, but her design was not (i) __________: Eliot hoped her fiction would have a (ii) __________ effect, spurting readers to show charity for those oppressed by the economic inequalities of her age.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　exclusively affective; galvanizing

答案：B, C　assiduousness; industry

#336 TC TRIPLE
Every few years, as a reviewer, one encounters a novel whose (i) __________ are so many in number, and so (ii) __________, that to correct them fully would produce a text that exceeded the novel itself in length. Faced with such a book, one wishes only to let it slip quietly to the seabed of culture, there to join thousands of other (iii) __________ books in their slow disintegration.Every few years, as a reviewer, one encounters a novel whose (i) __________ are so many in number, and so (ii) __________, that to correct them fully would produce a text that exceeded the novel itself in length. Faced with such a book, one wishes only to let it slip quietly to the seabed of culture, there to join thousands of other (iii) __________ books in their slow disintegration.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　ineptitudes; thoroughgoing; incompetent

novel: standard unit headword
