Literary salons and coffeehouses (i) __________ the class-based systems of social hierarchies prevalent at the time. Meritocratic appreciation of wit and brilliance, wherever it might issue, replaced (ii) __________ to established power.Literary salons and coffeehouses (i) __________ the class-based systems of social hierarchies prevalent at the time. Meritocratic appreciation of wit and brilliance, wherever it might issue, replaced (ii) __________ to established power.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　broke down; deference
issue
issue: standard unit headword
#338 SE
In his discussion of art, Ramachandran makes no distinction between the arousal value of a stimulus and its aesthetic value; he takes the emotional power of an artwork to be __________ that work's aesthetic quality.In his discussion of art, Ramachandran makes no distinction between the arousal value of a stimulus and its aesthetic value; he takes the emotional power of an artwork to be __________ that work's aesthetic quality.
答案：B, E　tantamount to; commensurate with

That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　superior to; closer to; apologize for

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：D, F　brevity; economy

She thinks we can (ii) __________ this without (iii) __________ the more exalted concepts of inspiration, the "inner voice," and so on. She, too, believes in those things.Flaherty understands that biological theories of artistic creation may (i) __________ people who cherish the idea that art comes from something other than the action of neurotransmitters, and she spends much time trying to shepherd us through the difficulty of accepting that the mind is actually the brain, a physical organ. She thinks we can (ii) __________ this without (iii) __________ the more exalted concepts of inspiration, the "inner voice," and so on. She, too, believes in those things.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　dismay; comprehend; discarding

Blank 1

答案：C, E　dispassionate inquiry; emotion

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　jettison; no special benefit for

#347 SE
The new technologies capable of rapidly propelling the economy forward over the next few decades may have the added __________ of helping the country cope with long-term fluctuations in economic activity.The new technologies capable of rapidly propelling the economy forward over the next few decades may have the added __________ of helping the country cope with long-term fluctuations in economic activity.
答案：A, D　attraction; benefit
fluctuation
fluctuation: standard unit headword
#348 TC TRIPLE
In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　conceal the artifice; intentional; mimicking
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　originated; remained largely unchanged

答案：D, F　dominance of; popularity of

#351 TC TRIPLE
Human brains are amazing organs but they are not perfect. Cognitive biases, as we know, sometimes produce errors. Yet the term "error" could be (i)__________ in that it suggests that there might be (ii)__________ way in which cognition should work. Such a binary opposition is (iii)__________.Human brains are amazing organs but they are not perfect. Cognitive biases, as we know, sometimes produce errors. Yet the term "error" could be (i)__________ in that it suggests that there might be (ii)__________ way in which cognition should work. Such a binary opposition is (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　problematic; a uniquely correct; reductive

There is little question that in primates there is (i) __________ between monogamous mating partnerships and extensive male care of infants. Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii) __________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.There is little question that in primates there is (i) __________ between monogamous mating partnerships and extensive male care of infants. Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii) __________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　an association; precluded by

答案：B, D　apogee; acme

Conversely, leaders who set goals that are credible extensions of current realities can seem unimaginative.The more (i) __________ the future goal advocated by an organization's leader, the more (ii) __________ it becomes in relation to the status quo, and therefore the greater will be the tendency of followers to perceive the leader as (iii) __________. Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　undertook; platitudinous

Here the review’s task is more (ii)__________: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)__________: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.

If she (ii) __________ the reasoning of proponents of one side, that did not mean that she rejected their position.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　inferable from; challenged

#360 TC TRIPLE
Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain

diminish: standard unit headword
#361 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　undermine; camouflage

quality: standard unit headword

Laughter, like speech, is a primarily human faculty, although both of these functions may also exist in a more __________ form in other primates.Laughter, like speech, is a primarily human faculty, although both of these functions may also exist in a more __________ form in other primates.
答案：A, B　rudimentary; elementary

#363 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword
#364 TC DOUBLE
As her fortunes diminished and her reputation ebbed, Hopkins' need for (i) __________ increased. Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) __________.As her fortunes diminished and her reputation ebbed, Hopkins' need for (i) __________ increased. Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　conviviality; anxiety
diminish
diminish: standard unit headword
#365 SE
The news that compatibility issues with the electronic device had been resolved was received with __________ by analysts and consumers, since they assumed that newer technologies would soon make the device obsolete.The news that compatibility issues with the electronic device had been resolved was received with __________ by analysts and consumers, since they assumed that newer technologies would soon make the device obsolete.
答案：A, B　indifference; apathy
issue
issue: standard unit headword
#366 TC TRIPLE
In a view of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.In a view of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　physical activity; detract from; augmented
diminish
diminish: standard unit headword
#367 TC DOUBLE
Nostalgia is to history as celebrity is to fame. Both nostalgia and celebrity are ways for the observer to (i) __________ things that are exotic, and that are characterized by their (ii) __________ the ordinary, and to make them, instead, familiar matters upon which the observer's own feelings can feed.Nostalgia is to history as celebrity is to fame. Both nostalgia and celebrity are ways for the observer to (i) __________ things that are exotic, and that are characterized by their (ii) __________ the ordinary, and to make them, instead, familiar matters upon which the observer's own feelings can feed.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　appropriate; difference from
characterize
characterize: standard unit headword
#368 SE
The article has been abridged for presentation to a nonspecialist audience, and the __________ by which this has been accomplished can be frustrating for any reader trying to reconstruct the argument in detail.The article has been abridged for presentation to a nonspecialist audience, and the __________ by which this has been accomplished can be frustrating for any reader trying to reconstruct the argument in detail.
答案：A, E　elisions; omissions
accomplished
accomplished: standard unit headword
#369 TC TRIPLE
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)__________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)__________ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)__________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)__________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)__________ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)__________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane
decode
decode: standard unit headword
#370 TC DOUBLE
The superficial agreement that characterizes the books' accounts of the incident is (i) __________, since it obscures the profound (ii) __________ that exist in their authors' understandings of the incident's significance.The superficial agreement that characterizes the books' accounts of the incident is (i) __________, since it obscures the profound (ii) __________ that exist in their authors' understandings of the incident's significance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　misleading; differences
characterize
characterize: standard unit headword
#371 SE
Although automated agricultural technology boosts yields and profits, its high degree of prescriptiveness diminishes the role of individual farmers by making their __________ almost irrelevant.Although automated agricultural technology boosts yields and profits, its high degree of prescriptiveness diminishes the role of individual farmers by making their __________ almost irrelevant.
答案：C, E　judgment; discretion
diminish
diminish: standard unit headword
#372 TC TRIPLE
The historian’s narrative of the dynasty’s decline is (i)__________, because though there is nothing (ii)__________ the account, large parts of it rely on (iii)__________.The historian’s narrative of the dynasty’s decline is (i)__________, because though there is nothing (ii)__________ the account, large parts of it rely on (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　questionable; inherently implausible about; conjecture
document
document: standard unit headword
#373 TC DOUBLE
The technology of integrated circuits inaugurated a new era of electronics, which can be characterized by saying that any electronic circuit, however (i) __________, that can be designed can also be made reliably and cheaply, provided it is manufactured and sold in sufficient numbers to (ii) __________ the cost of design and production.The technology of integrated circuits inaugurated a new era of electronics, which can be characterized by saying that any electronic circuit, however (i) __________, that can be designed can also be made reliably and cheaply, provided it is manufactured and sold in sufficient numbers to (ii) __________ the cost of design and production.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　complicated; recover
characterize
characterize: standard unit headword
#374 SE
In contrast to the novel's scenic realism and precise characterization is its persistent philosophical __________.In contrast to the novel's scenic realism and precise characterization is its persistent philosophical __________.
答案：B, C　abstraction; generality
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#375 TC TRIPLE
Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from
supplant
supplant: standard unit headword
#376 TC DOUBLE
Science is so good at what it does that many people feel a (i) __________ to apply it to social and political issues, yet experience shows that science may add little to or even (ii) __________ social and political questions.Science is so good at what it does that many people feel a (i) __________ to apply it to social and political issues, yet experience shows that science may add little to or even (ii) __________ social and political questions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　temptation; occlude
issue
issue: standard unit headword
#377 SE
In Grey Gardens, the Maysles brothers defy the conventions of pure documentary technique by inserting themselves into the picture, __________ that serves to personalize the movie.In Grey Gardens, the Maysles brothers defy the conventions of pure documentary technique by inserting themselves into the picture, __________ that serves to personalize the movie.
答案：C, F　a transgression; a breach
documentary
documentary: standard unit headword
#378 TC TRIPLE
The historian's narrative of the dynasty's decline is (i)__________, because though there is nothing (ii)__________ the account, largely parts of it rely on (iii)__________.The historian's narrative of the dynasty's decline is (i)__________, because though there is nothing (ii)__________ the account, largely parts of it rely on (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　questionable; inherently implausible about; conjecture
document
document: standard unit headword
#379 TC DOUBLE
Wald's decision to tell Rosetta Tharpe's story to a wider audience in Shout, Sister, Shout! (i) __________ the rigor of the book's scholarship or the acuity of its theory. The scholarly quality of the work is (ii) __________ on every page, and yet it is not a dry, specialist academic tome.Wald's decision to tell Rosetta Tharpe's story to a wider audience in Shout, Sister, Shout! (i) __________ the rigor of the book's scholarship or the acuity of its theory. The scholarly quality of the work is (ii) __________ on every page, and yet it is not a dry, specialist academic tome.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　does not compromise; evident
quality
quality: standard unit headword
#380 SE
The early universe was __________, but no longer: galaxies, galaxy clusters, and stars punctuate the expanse of empty space, staking their uneven distribution throughout the firmament.The early universe was __________, but no longer: galaxies, galaxy clusters, and stars punctuate the expanse of empty space, staking their uneven distribution throughout the firmament.
答案：C, E　uniform; homogeneous
cluster
cluster: standard unit headword

In a review of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.In a review of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　physical activity; detract from; augmented

diminish: standard unit headword
#382 TC DOUBLE
The scarcity of craters on the surface of Charon, one of Pluto's moons, suggests that changes on the moon's surface have (i) __________ the (ii) __________ previous asteroid collisions.The scarcity of craters on the surface of Charon, one of Pluto's moons, suggests that changes on the moon's surface have (i) __________ the (ii) __________ previous asteroid collisions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　erased; evidence of
scarcity
scarcity: standard unit headword
#383 SE
Augustus, the first emperor of Rome, was celebrated for his moderation and temperate living, qualities that would distinguish him from so many of his __________ successors.Augustus, the first emperor of Rome, was celebrated for his moderation and temperate living, qualities that would distinguish him from so many of his __________ successors.
答案：B, D　sybaritic; hedonistic
quality
quality: standard unit headword
#384 TC TRIPLE
Early practitioners of the natural sciences developed methods to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)__________ research.Early practitioners of the natural sciences developed methods to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)__________ research.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　restrain; incursions of; corrupt
justify
justify: standard unit headword
#385 SE
The senator complained that the president's argument against the provision was __________, gaining its apparent plausibility from a distorted presentation of the relevant data.The senator complained that the president's argument against the provision was __________, gaining its apparent plausibility from a distorted presentation of the relevant data.
答案：B, C　specious; deceptive
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword
#386 TC DOUBLE
The bankruptcy expert has long argued that the entrepreneurial spirit in the United States is (i) __________ the practice that we, as a society, have had of treating risk takers (ii) __________ when they fail. Reweighting the system to favor creditors could diminish that spirit, she warns, and make it harder for entrepreneurs to try again after a failure.The bankruptcy expert has long argued that the entrepreneurial spirit in the United States is (i) __________ the practice that we, as a society, have had of treating risk takers (ii) __________ when they fail. Reweighting the system to favor creditors could diminish that spirit, she warns, and make it harder for entrepreneurs to try again after a failure.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　fostered by; charitably
diminish
diminish: standard unit headword
#387 TC TRIPLE
The new art museum's (i)__________ building augurs well for that ambitious institution because it speaks of (ii)__________ contemporary architecture on the part of the board of directors that may (iii)__________ equal astuteness about contemporary art.The new art museum's (i)__________ building augurs well for that ambitious institution because it speaks of (ii)__________ contemporary architecture on the part of the board of directors that may (iii)__________ equal astuteness about contemporary art.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　outstanding; a discernment about; promise
supplant
supplant: standard unit headword
#388 SE
Duke Ellington managed his career with extreme deliberateness: nothing was __________, and even apparent compromises or trade-offs were actually artful accommodations leading to eventual triumphs.Duke Ellington managed his career with extreme deliberateness: nothing was __________, and even apparent compromises or trade-offs were actually artful accommodations leading to eventual triumphs.
答案：B, D　fortuitous; accidental
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword
#389 TC DOUBLE
Due to the extraordinary circumstances, British business owners found themselves in a (i)__________ position during the Second World War, forced to accept interference from the government and to acquiesce to (ii)__________ role for labor unions in negotiating the terms and conditions of the employment.Due to the extraordinary circumstances, British business owners found themselves in a (i)__________ position during the Second World War, forced to accept interference from the government and to acquiesce to (ii)__________ role for labor unions in negotiating the terms and conditions of the employment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　defensive; an enhanced
diminish
diminish: standard unit headword
#390 TC TRIPLE
The characters in this comic strip fret about the (i)__________ of their "little counterculture lives", especially when terrible things are happening in the world, but the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)__________ in ways that do not seem (iii)__________ at all. Real things happen here--births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil unions--and they matter.The characters in this comic strip fret about the (i)__________ of their "little counterculture lives", especially when terrible things are happening in the world, but the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)__________ in ways that do not seem (iii)__________ at all. Real things happen here--births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil unions--and they matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　triviality; resonate; inconsequential
stagnate
stagnate: standard unit headword
#391 SE
The awards and critical acclaim for the author's new novel were not unexpected, as her previous works had already secured their author a reputation for literary __________.The awards and critical acclaim for the author's new novel were not unexpected, as her previous works had already secured their author a reputation for literary __________.
答案：B, E　virtuosity; mastery
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#392 TC DOUBLE
The author clearly supports the causes he writes about, but he is more a narrator than (i)__________. Some say he should have included more (ii)__________, but he is wise to let the fact speak for themselves. They are complex enough to prompt many kinds of interpretation, and he would be bogged down in the complicated tale if he tried to adjudicate all of their competing claims.The author clearly supports the causes he writes about, but he is more a narrator than (i)__________. Some say he should have included more (ii)__________, but he is wise to let the fact speak for themselves. They are complex enough to prompt many kinds of interpretation, and he would be bogged down in the complicated tale if he tried to adjudicate all of their competing claims.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　an advocate; analysis of events
adversary
adversary: standard unit headword
#393 TC TRIPLE
Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievements of the ESA in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievements of the ESA in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain
diminish
diminish: standard unit headword
#394 SE
During the eighteenth century, improvements in their material circumstances did not necessarily mean expanded independence for women of elite families and, arguably, the social conventions of gentility __________ more of their time and energy.During the eighteenth century, improvements in their material circumstances did not necessarily mean expanded independence for women of elite families and, arguably, the social conventions of gentility __________ more of their time and energy.
答案：C, E　demanded; exacted
rationalizejustify
rationalize: standard unit headword | justify: standard unit headword
#395 TC DOUBLE
Although it is not uncommon for journalists to portray political inexperience on the part of public officials as an (i)__________, it was nevertheless surprising when members of the press treated the new senator's obvious (ii)__________ as an extraordinary virtue.Although it is not uncommon for journalists to portray political inexperience on the part of public officials as an (i)__________, it was nevertheless surprising when members of the press treated the new senator's obvious (ii)__________ as an extraordinary virtue.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　advantage; naïveté
acumen
acumen: standard unit headword
#396 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword
#397 SE
Aerial viewings of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechuan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appears crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.Aerial viewings of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechuan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appears crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.
答案：A, C　reveal; manifest
diminishjustify

Memoirs are inherently (i)__________, but Larry McMurtry’s volume of reminiscences about his life with books—not as a novelist but as a reader and bookstore owner—is especially (ii)__________: nearly every page sounds a note of farewell to an age of books that he sees as passing.Memoirs are inherently (i)__________, but Larry McMurtry’s volume of reminiscences about his life with books—not as a novelist but as a reader and bookstore owner—is especially (ii)__________: nearly every page sounds a note of farewell to an age of books that he sees as passing.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　wistful; valedictory
whimsical

#399 TC TRIPLE
Fifty pages of footnotes, some of them presenting quite lengthy bibliographies, suggest that very few pertinent sources on the Black Arts movement in literature have (i)__________ Thompson's search; (ii)__________, the text makes it clear that the author's examination of these sources has been similarly (iii)__________.Fifty pages of footnotes, some of them presenting quite lengthy bibliographies, suggest that very few pertinent sources on the Black Arts movement in literature have (i)__________ Thompson's search; (ii)__________, the text makes it clear that the author's examination of these sources has been similarly (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　eluded; moreover; exhaustive
characterize
characterize: standard unit headword
#400 SE
Even overt deficiencies in the author’s work have not __________ its respectful reception by most modern critics.Even overt deficiencies in the author’s work have not __________ its respectful reception by most modern critics.
答案：B, D　hindered; inhibited
proscribe
proscribe: standard unit headword
#401 TC DOUBLE
Although the dispute between the sisters lasted all summer, Megan remained (i) __________ and Lauren was equally (ii) __________.Although the dispute between the sisters lasted all summer, Megan remained (i) __________ and Lauren was equally (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　intransigent; uncompromising
munificent
munificent: standard unit headword

Many popular musicians have (i)__________ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.Many popular musicians have (i)__________ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　incorporated; recast; balk at
energize
energize: standard unit headword
#403 SE
Since it was committed to the idea of science as an international, politically neutral enterprise, the Royal Society of London refused to __________ members from enemy nations during world wars of the twentieth century.Since it was committed to the idea of science as an international, politically neutral enterprise, the Royal Society of London refused to __________ members from enemy nations during world wars of the twentieth century.
答案：B, D　expel; oust
betray
betray: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　defensive; an enhanced

#405 TC TRIPLE
The wealthy donor was known for his annual acts of (i) __________ throughout the community, but even more (ii) __________ was the fact that he was willing to get his hands dirty and serve the needy through hard physical labor as well as through (iii) __________ and gifts.The wealthy donor was known for his annual acts of (i) __________ throughout the community, but even more (ii) __________ was the fact that he was willing to get his hands dirty and serve the needy through hard physical labor as well as through (iii) __________ and gifts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　largesse; laudable; endowments
quality
quality: standard unit headword
#406 SE
Even if the story now seems a surprisingly innocuous overture to the author’s later, more fully developed narrations, it __________ some of the key traits of those bleaker tales.Even if the story now seems a surprisingly innocuous overture to the author’s later, more fully developed narrations, it __________ some of the key traits of those bleaker tales.
答案：D, F　anticipates; prefigures
belie
belie: standard unit headword
#407 TC DOUBLE
The material covered in this article has been (i) __________ in previous publications, and since currently neglected areas remained unexplored, the article contains no (ii) __________.The material covered in this article has been (i) __________ in previous publications, and since currently neglected areas remained unexplored, the article contains no (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　scrutinized; revelations
skirt
skirt: standard unit headword
#408 TC TRIPLE
Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent
document
document: standard unit headword
#409 SE
Even the cleverest use of time management techniques is powerless to __________ sum of minutes in a person’s life, so people squeeze as much as they could into each one of them.Even the cleverest use of time management techniques is powerless to __________ sum of minutes in a person’s life, so people squeeze as much as they could into each one of them.
答案：C, F　augment; extend
justify
justify: standard unit headword

The author clearly supports the causes he writes about, but he is more a narrator than (i) __________. Some say he should have included more (ii) __________, but he is wise to let the facts speak for themselves. They are complex enough to prompt many kinds of interpretation, and he would bog down the complicated tale if he tried to adjudicate all of their competing claims.The author clearly supports the causes he writes about, but he is more a narrator than (i) __________. Some say he should have included more (ii) __________, but he is wise to let the facts speak for themselves. They are complex enough to prompt many kinds of interpretation, and he would bog down the complicated tale if he tried to adjudicate all of their competing claims.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　an advocate; analysis of events
adversary
adversary: standard unit headword
#411 TC TRIPLE
The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　preclude; prudent; minimize
diminish
diminish: standard unit headword
#412 SE
Since some contemporary western dietitians believe that the only function of food is to provide nourishment, these dietitians view an emphasis on the aesthetic dimension of the culinary art as __________.Since some contemporary western dietitians believe that the only function of food is to provide nourishment, these dietitians view an emphasis on the aesthetic dimension of the culinary art as __________.
答案：B, D　unwarranted; groundless
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#413 TC DOUBLE
The genius of the scientific method is that it (i) __________ the dictum of Aristotle that the goal of science is knowledge of the ultimate cause of things. True science, we now know, advances human knowledge by (ii) __________ ultimate causes and focusing instead on the testing of empirical hypotheses.The genius of the scientific method is that it (i) __________ the dictum of Aristotle that the goal of science is knowledge of the ultimate cause of things. True science, we now know, advances human knowledge by (ii) __________ ultimate causes and focusing instead on the testing of empirical hypotheses.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　jettisons; ignoring
qualify
qualify: standard unit headword
#414 TC TRIPLE
People accustomed to thinking that the human lifespan (i) __________ the outer bounds of animal longevity tend to dismiss tales of musket balls being found in the shells of living turtles. Samantha Romney, however, argues that while such stories may be (ii) __________, some turtles do indeed exhibit a phenomenon known as "negligible (iii) __________," showing no signs of aging even as they pass the two-century mark.People accustomed to thinking that the human lifespan (i) __________ the outer bounds of animal longevity tend to dismiss tales of musket balls being found in the shells of living turtles. Samantha Romney, however, argues that while such stories may be (ii) __________, some turtles do indeed exhibit a phenomenon known as "negligible (iii) __________," showing no signs of aging even as they pass the two-century mark.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　demarcates; apocryphal; senescence
belie
belie: standard unit headword
#415 SE
While it is always clear that the author’s message is heartfelt, it is mostly buried by shortcomings of style, organization and production, although the book does become more __________ toward the end.While it is always clear that the author’s message is heartfelt, it is mostly buried by shortcomings of style, organization and production, although the book does become more __________ toward the end.
答案：B, D　intelligible; readable
voluble
voluble: standard unit headword
#416 TC DOUBLE
In aquatic environments, the herbicide atrazine is more likely to (i) __________ developing amphibians when it is highly diluted than when it is much more concentrated, a new study suggests. Although counterintuitive, the finding is (ii) __________ some past research on atrazine and studies showing that other hormonally active compounds are most damaging at trace concentrations.In aquatic environments, the herbicide atrazine is more likely to (i) __________ developing amphibians when it is highly diluted than when it is much more concentrated, a new study suggests. Although counterintuitive, the finding is (ii) __________ some past research on atrazine and studies showing that other hormonally active compounds are most damaging at trace concentrations.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　kill; consistent with
supplant
supplant: standard unit headword
#417 TC TRIPLE
Persian prose writers from the second half of the twelfth century onward were characteristically (i)__________ the literary form of their works, a fact that some scholars have perceived as a derogation of those works' content. It may be better interpreted as (ii)__________ the development of an awareness of authorship, for the awareness of authorship (iii)__________ the awareness of form.Persian prose writers from the second half of the twelfth century onward were characteristically (i)__________ the literary form of their works, a fact that some scholars have perceived as a derogation of those works' content. It may be better interpreted as (ii)__________ the development of an awareness of authorship, for the awareness of authorship (iii)__________ the awareness of form.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　preoccupied with; an indication of; evolves through
justification
justification: standard unit headword
#418 SE
Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.
答案：A, B　manifest; reveal

diminish: standard unit headword
#419 TC DOUBLE
Although it is not uncommon for journalists to portray political inexperience on the part of public officials as an (i) __________, it was nevertheless surprising when members of the press treated the new senator's obvious (ii) __________ as an extraordinary virtue.Although it is not uncommon for journalists to portray political inexperience on the part of public officials as an (i) __________, it was nevertheless surprising when members of the press treated the new senator's obvious (ii) __________ as an extraordinary virtue.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　impediment; naiveté

acumen: standard unit headword
#420 TC TRIPLE
Since the 1920s, historical fiction writers in China have emancipated the genre from the traditional notion that (i)__________ was the ultimate goal of history writing. Yet the traditional commitment to (ii)__________ was not simply (iii)__________: this new genre was expected to capture the essence of historical truth even as it allowed space for the writer's imagination.Since the 1920s, historical fiction writers in China have emancipated the genre from the traditional notion that (i)__________ was the ultimate goal of history writing. Yet the traditional commitment to (ii)__________ was not simply (iii)__________: this new genre was expected to capture the essence of historical truth even as it allowed space for the writer's imagination.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　factuality; veracity; jettisoned
rationalize
rationalize: standard unit headword
#421 SE
There are great __________ in countries’ greenhouse gas emissions, especially in per capita terms; while the United States and China are similar in aggregate emissions, United States’ per capita emissions are a huge multiple of China’s.There are great __________ in countries’ greenhouse gas emissions, especially in per capita terms; while the United States and China are similar in aggregate emissions, United States’ per capita emissions are a huge multiple of China’s.
答案：B, E　disparities; variances
fluctuation
fluctuation: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　venerated; stunned

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　rote work; extrapolation; inferred

#424 SE

答案：C, E　private; insular

unerring: standard unit headword
#425 TC DOUBLE
Any number of mysteries to which individual scholars of Athenian history have devoted whole careers are addressed by Ober, and mostly successfully. This will cause some (i) __________ among scholars who have worked for years on a particular problem only to see another scholar suddenly (ii) __________ it.Any number of mysteries to which individual scholars of Athenian history have devoted whole careers are addressed by Ober, and mostly successfully. This will cause some (i) __________ among scholars who have worked for years on a particular problem only to see another scholar suddenly (ii) __________ it.

Blank 2
答案：A, F　chagrin; resolve

But most state labels have a predominantly (i)__________ character. Labels such as failed state or democratic state tend to be accepted only by those who (ii)__________ the assumptions that (iii)__________ such a marker.Sometimes the criteria that are used to categorize nation-states are purely factual: for example, the denotation of a state as a coastal state or an inland state. But most state labels have a predominantly (i)__________ character. Labels such as failed state or democratic state tend to be accepted only by those who (ii)__________ the assumptions that (iii)__________ such a marker.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　evaluative; share; constitute the basis of

functional: standard unit headword

To call Kermode the finest English critic of his generation would be a __________ compliment, since not many of its population are professionally engaged in literary criticism.To call Kermode the finest English critic of his generation would be a __________ compliment, since not many of its population are professionally engaged in literary criticism.
答案：C, E　paltry; meager

plausible: standard unit headword

The substantial size and weight of the specialized products produced by Architectural Pottery helped (ii) __________ the company, because it was uneconomical for foreign companies to ship similarly large objects to California.As cheaper imports drove most California potteries out of business during the 1950s, one company (i) __________. The substantial size and weight of the specialized products produced by Architectural Pottery helped (ii) __________ the company, because it was uneconomical for foreign companies to ship similarly large objects to California.

Blank 2
答案：C, E　flourished; insulate

#429 TC TRIPLE
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools--quantum mechanics and streams of individual photons--to shut out (i) __________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii) __________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools--quantum mechanics and streams of individual photons--to shut out (i) __________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii) __________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane

答案：A, B　abound; proliferate

stagnate: standard unit headword

The prime minister's spokesman faces a difficult task in defending his boss from what he sees as the (i) __________ of a decidedly prejudiced press. To make his defense effectively he will certainly have to give specific examples of the media coverage he finds so (ii) __________.The prime minister's spokesman faces a difficult task in defending his boss from what he sees as the (i) __________ of a decidedly prejudiced press. To make his defense effectively he will certainly have to give specific examples of the media coverage he finds so (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　lowly calumnies; objectionable

The longer the migratory route, the more fat migrating birds must accumulate as fuel for their flight. The gain in body mass would make birds obese by human standards, and should render them unable to fly, but this gain is (i)__________ premigratory (ii)__________ of digestive organs, inactive during migration, and by the (iii)__________ of the muscles that power the flight.The longer the migratory route, the more fat migrating birds must accumulate as fuel for their flight. The gain in body mass would make birds obese by human standards, and should render them unable to fly, but this gain is (i)__________ premigratory (ii)__________ of digestive organs, inactive during migration, and by the (iii)__________ of the muscles that power the flight.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　partially offset by; shrinking; strengthening

#433 SE

答案：C, E　obviate; displace

She was by nature (ii) __________.

Blank 2

In his own writings and pronouncements as well as in firsthand accounts offered by others, Prajadhipok consistently emerges as (ii) __________ and even-tempered, though (iii) __________.

答案：C, E, G　facile; thoughtful; unexceptional

答案：B, D　speculative; conjectural

For example, in one study in which college students selected their favorite poster from a set, those asked to explain their selection ended up less (iii) __________ their choices than those who were not asked. Researchers concluded that thinking of reasons interferes with people's ability to access gut-level reactions that are crucial to sound decision making.Thinking (i) __________ about one's choices can sometimes (ii) __________ decision making. For example, in one study in which college students selected their favorite poster from a set, those asked to explain their selection ended up less (iii) __________ their choices than those who were not asked. Researchers concluded that thinking of reasons interferes with people's ability to access gut-level reactions that are crucial to sound decision making.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　explicitly; reduce satisfaction with; happy with

#443 TC TRIPLE
The general statement that all objects are (i)__________ by gravity (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________. In reality, if a feather and a lump of iron were dropped at the same time from the same place, we should not expect them to hit the floor at the same time. In order to reconcile the general statement with real-world phenomena, we must take into consideration all of the forces that act upon objects.The general statement that all objects are (i)__________ by gravity (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________. In reality, if a feather and a lump of iron were dropped at the same time from the same place, we should not expect them to hit the floor at the same time. In order to reconcile the general statement with real-world phenomena, we must take into consideration all of the forces that act upon objects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　accelerated; at the same rate; self-evident
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#444 TC DOUBLE
Unlike, say, locusts, which simply raze entire fields, stinkbugs wreak their havoc (i) __________. The injury they do to corn, for instance, is (ii) __________ the ear is husked, at which point certain kernels--the ones into which a stinkbug stuck its pointy mouth--will reveal themselves to be sunken and brown.Unlike, say, locusts, which simply raze entire fields, stinkbugs wreak their havoc (i) __________. The injury they do to corn, for instance, is (ii) __________ the ear is husked, at which point certain kernels--the ones into which a stinkbug stuck its pointy mouth--will reveal themselves to be sunken and brown.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　insidiously; invisible until

答案：B, F　perspicacity; discernment

Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.

答案：A, E, I　alarming; reassuring; unexceptional

Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)__________, a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)__________ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)__________ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deferential; radical; profound shift

Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) __________ before the scientific process has been (iii) __________.Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) __________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) __________ before the scientific process has been (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　inverted; defend her findings; concluded

#460 TC DOUBLE
Accepting that the boundaries that define an ecosystem are, to an extent, (i) __________ opens up the possibility that people might have competing but equally (ii) __________ ideas of what the components of an ecosystem should be and where the boundaries between ecosystems should lie.Accepting that the boundaries that define an ecosystem are, to an extent, (i) __________ opens up the possibility that people might have competing but equally (ii) __________ ideas of what the components of an ecosystem should be and where the boundaries between ecosystems should lie.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　imagined; defensible

#461 SE
Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong--and, consequently, we are lacking in any __________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong--and, consequently, we are lacking in any __________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：A, C　incisive; trenchant
tantalize
tantalize: standard unit headword
#462 TC TRIPLE
In 1960, journalist Murray Kempton argued that sociologists, seeking to (i) __________ of meaningful research findings, used (ii) __________ language to (iii) __________ the fraudulence of their discipline's claims to scientific status.In 1960, journalist Murray Kempton argued that sociologists, seeking to (i) __________ of meaningful research findings, used (ii) __________ language to (iii) __________ the fraudulence of their discipline's claims to scientific status.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　obscure the paucity; recondite; disguise
quotidian
quotidian: standard unit headword
#463 SE
At first glance, the new CEO stands in stark contrast to his predecessor: while Simpkins came across as a scolding headmaster, his successor appears __________.At first glance, the new CEO stands in stark contrast to his predecessor: while Simpkins came across as a scolding headmaster, his successor appears __________.
答案：A, D　blithe; winsome
scrupulous
scrupulous: standard unit headword
#464 TC DOUBLE
The quest for new drugs to deter the aging process represents a new faith among some researchers that aging is not immutable. This (i) __________, however, is not (ii) __________. Evolutionary biologists have strong reasons to suppose that the human life span cannot be altered in any easy way.The quest for new drugs to deter the aging process represents a new faith among some researchers that aging is not immutable. This (i) __________, however, is not (ii) __________. Evolutionary biologists have strong reasons to suppose that the human life span cannot be altered in any easy way.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　optimism; fully shared
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword
#465 TC TRIPLE
It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (iii) __________ their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (iii) __________ their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　exaggerated expectations; realism; preclude
diminish
diminish: standard unit headword
#466 SE
Producers are concerned about Broadway theater's economic straits, which, even setting aside current woes, could __________ difficulty raising money for future productions.Producers are concerned about Broadway theater's economic straits, which, even setting aside current woes, could __________ difficulty raising money for future productions.
答案：A, B　presage; foretell
belie
belie: standard unit headword
#467 TC DOUBLE
When an ideologue of one persuasion reverses course and embraces the views of erstwhile opponents, those whose views the convert adopts may feel vindicated. Still, one wants a person making a political about-face to (i) __________ of his or her previous allegiances: a reversal without a (ii) __________ is less than satisfying.When an ideologue of one persuasion reverses course and embraces the views of erstwhile opponents, those whose views the convert adopts may feel vindicated. Still, one wants a person making a political about-face to (i) __________ of his or her previous allegiances: a reversal without a (ii) __________ is less than satisfying.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　acknowledge the erroneousness; recantation
rationalize
rationalize: standard unit headword
#468 TC TRIPLE
Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty
accomplished
accomplished: standard unit headword
#469 SE
Takako Saito's artistic development has been difficult for scholars to study, in part because Saito's __________ way of life naturally left records of her activities scattered across different languages and places.Takako Saito's artistic development has been difficult for scholars to study, in part because Saito's __________ way of life naturally left records of her activities scattered across different languages and places.
答案：C, E　itinerant; peripatetic
quixotic
quixotic: standard unit headword
#470 TC TRIPLE
A favorite tactic of George Eliot's irresponsibly (i) __________ biographer is to hoist an alluring psychological flag concerning Eliot and then scrupulously take it down again, because the evidence will not serve to (ii) __________. But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.A favorite tactic of George Eliot's irresponsibly (i) __________ biographer is to hoist an alluring psychological flag concerning Eliot and then scrupulously take it down again, because the evidence will not serve to (ii) __________. But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　speculative; keep it up; gratuitous
justify
justify: standard unit headword
#471 TC DOUBLE
Although there is certainly no (i) __________ for overcoming (ii) __________ mind-set among students, there are numerous materials, approaches, and exercises that, if used creatively, can help students gain glimpses of how other individuals think of themselves and see their worlds.Although there is certainly no (i) __________ for overcoming (ii) __________ mind-set among students, there are numerous materials, approaches, and exercises that, if used creatively, can help students gain glimpses of how other individuals think of themselves and see their worlds.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　panacea; an insular
justification
justification: standard unit headword
#472 SE
While lunar geology may appear to be a relatively __________ area of planetary science, it is important to realize that the Moon's surface and interior retain records that shed light on the histories of all terrestrial planets.While lunar geology may appear to be a relatively __________ area of planetary science, it is important to realize that the Moon's surface and interior retain records that shed light on the histories of all terrestrial planets.
答案：A, C　parochial; narrow
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#473 TC TRIPLE
Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　deflects; untrue; gauge its veracity
document
document: standard unit headword
#474 TC DOUBLE
Many nineteenth-century women writers in the United States encountered fierce criticism for stepping beyond what society considered their designated sphere. Early in her career as an author, Lydia Maria Child sought to (i) __________ such (ii) __________ by keeping her authorship secret.Many nineteenth-century women writers in the United States encountered fierce criticism for stepping beyond what society considered their designated sphere. Early in her career as an author, Lydia Maria Child sought to (i) __________ such (ii) __________ by keeping her authorship secret.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　evade; opprobrium
belie
belie: standard unit headword
#475 SE
The geometry student found the proposition she was supposed to demonstrate to be so __________ as to obviate demonstration: since opposite angles were self-evidently equal, there was no need for a proof.The geometry student found the proposition she was supposed to demonstrate to be so __________ as to obviate demonstration: since opposite angles were self-evidently equal, there was no need for a proof.
答案：B, E　patent; unequivocal
plausible
plausible: standard unit headword

While Hassler's trademark (i) __________ is sometimes useful in (ii) __________ an institution entrenched in complacency into changing its ways, such brusqueness is more often counterproductive, tending to (iii) __________ people.While Hassler's trademark (i) __________ is sometimes useful in (ii) __________ an institution entrenched in complacency into changing its ways, such brusqueness is more often counterproductive, tending to (iii) __________ people.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　bluntness; prodding; alienate

While acknowledging behaviors the Prime Minister took in order to remain in office were (i) __________, some politicians nevertheless believed this small amount of (ii) __________ was justified to keep reforming government in office.While acknowledging behaviors the Prime Minister took in order to remain in office were (i) __________, some politicians nevertheless believed this small amount of (ii) __________ was justified to keep reforming government in office.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　unethical; skullduggery

Because urban soils are formed from diverse, often non-soil, materials and have gone through variable periods of development, the normal heterogeneity encountered in wetland soils may be greatly __________ in urban wetlands.Because urban soils are formed from diverse, often non-soil, materials and have gone through variable periods of development, the normal heterogeneity encountered in wetland soils may be greatly __________ in urban wetlands.
答案：E, F　magnified; intensified

In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　the seminal; concedes; original

eccentric: standard unit headword

Blank 1

#482 TC TRIPLE
In interviews, voters (i) __________ the (ii) __________ tone of the race, saying that the two candidates should do a better job explaining their plans rather than exchanging volleys in misleading television ads. Though each candidate claims to act independently in matters of principle, polls indicate that voters overwhelmingly view both as (iii) __________ of party bosses.In interviews, voters (i) __________ the (ii) __________ tone of the race, saying that the two candidates should do a better job explaining their plans rather than exchanging volleys in misleading television ads. Though each candidate claims to act independently in matters of principle, polls indicate that voters overwhelmingly view both as (iii) __________ of party bosses.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　groused about; caustic; minions

#483 TC DOUBLE
In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle's corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i) __________ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii) __________ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle's corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i) __________ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii) __________ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　unique; can be traced to
mechanisticjustify
mechanistic: standard unit headword | justify: standard unit headword
#484 SE
Because they require abstraction and generalization, many theories end up __________ practical relevance as they fail to capture the richness and complexity of phenomena encountered in real settings.Because they require abstraction and generalization, many theories end up __________ practical relevance as they fail to capture the richness and complexity of phenomena encountered in real settings.
答案：A, E　lacking; wanting
supplant
supplant: standard unit headword
#485 TC TRIPLE
It is an accepted assumption of the theater that every playwright has an unproduced play stuck away in a drawer somewhere. The same assumption holds that such plays are usually youthful efforts and that all urges to take them out of the drawer should be (i) __________, lest they bring (ii) __________ to the playwright and (iii) __________ to admirers.It is an accepted assumption of the theater that every playwright has an unproduced play stuck away in a drawer somewhere. The same assumption holds that such plays are usually youthful efforts and that all urges to take them out of the drawer should be (i) __________, lest they bring (ii) __________ to the playwright and (iii) __________ to admirers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　repressed; embarrassment; distress
qualify
qualify: standard unit headword
#486 SE
Heidegger's philosophy of science tends to give prominence to visuals and visualization because for Heidegger, truth-including scientific truth-is the __________ of unconcealment: it is the lifting of a veil.Heidegger's philosophy of science tends to give prominence to visuals and visualization because for Heidegger, truth-including scientific truth-is the __________ of unconcealment: it is the lifting of a veil.
答案：A, F　consequence; result

#487 TC TRIPLE
Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific works validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)__________ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific works validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)__________ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed

novel: standard unit headword | justify: standard unit headword

答案：B, C　a cosmopolitan; an inclusive

答案：D, F　soft; malleable

(i) __________ conventional wisdom, they inspire scholars to (ii) __________ lines of inquiry virtually unimagined before their publication. Other works, valuable in their own way, fill in gaps by challenging readers to rethink issues that had been thought to be (iii) __________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　Revising; pursue; resolved

答案：B, E　contemplative; ruminative

Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　monster; prickly; sympathetic

#494 SE
Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.
答案：C, F　prudent; circumspect

Seawater will have (ii) __________ coastal rivers, lakes, and groundwater, turning (iii) __________ water into brackish water.A few decades from now, if the sea level rises as most scientists expect it to, fresh, high-quality drinking water will be much more (i) __________ within 60 miles of the ocean than it is now. Seawater will have (ii) __________ coastal rivers, lakes, and groundwater, turning (iii) __________ water into brackish water.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　scarce; tainted; potable

At the turn of the twentieth century, the status of dinosaurs changed dramatically in the United States; fascination with them, previously __________ passion, became widespread.At the turn of the twentieth century, the status of dinosaurs changed dramatically in the United States; fascination with them, previously __________ passion, became widespread.
答案：A, F　an esoteric; an arcane

#497 SE
Leaders within the Native American culture known as Fort Ancient differed from those of the neighboring Mississippian groups, whose authority was often __________; indeed, one expert has described the Fort Ancient society as intensely egalitarian.Leaders within the Native American culture known as Fort Ancient differed from those of the neighboring Mississippian groups, whose authority was often __________; indeed, one expert has described the Fort Ancient society as intensely egalitarian.
答案：D, F　coercive; imperious

The concept of replicability begins with the idea that the pursuit of scientific truth is not __________: researchers who make claims must allow others to test those claims.The concept of replicability begins with the idea that the pursuit of scientific truth is not __________: researchers who make claims must allow others to test those claims.
答案：C, E　private; insular

答案：A, C　slam; vilify

In her early years as a theater critic, Willa Cather was __________, veering between ecstatic raves and brutal takedowns.In her early years as a theater critic, Willa Cather was __________, veering between ecstatic raves and brutal takedowns.
答案：C, E　mercurial; volatile
voluble

#501 SE
With Duncan's ambitious study, Chesnutt appears finally to have outgrown the __________ treatment he once received from literary historians and critics for whom he typically served as a merely transitional figure in the African American tradition.With Duncan's ambitious study, Chesnutt appears finally to have outgrown the __________ treatment he once received from literary historians and critics for whom he typically served as a merely transitional figure in the African American tradition.
答案：A, F　cursory; perfunctory

Memory is __________ record of the past; it is a rough sketch that can be modified even by the simple act of telling the story.Memory is __________ record of the past; it is a rough sketch that can be modified even by the simple act of telling the story.
答案：A, E　a mutable; an alterable

答案：A, E　fragility; impermanence

#504 SE
The author's description of the __________ contamination of ocean water with plastic materials--and the resulting threat to marine life--constitutes a valuable lesson in the urgency of managing trash more responsibly than we currently do.The author's description of the __________ contamination of ocean water with plastic materials--and the resulting threat to marine life--constitutes a valuable lesson in the urgency of managing trash more responsibly than we currently do.
答案：B, D　ubiquitous; pervasive

diminish: standard unit headword
#505 SE
Publicity around the celebrities’ donations to charity is often greeted with cynicism, but a study of the celebrities’ donation shows that they do __________ other donation.Publicity around the celebrities’ donations to charity is often greeted with cynicism, but a study of the celebrities’ donation shows that they do __________ other donation.
答案：C, D　draw; elicit

答案：B, C　proclaimed; professed

#507 SE
The town’s air was consistently __________: depending on the breeze, one might be greeted with the sour effluvia of twenty breweries, choking fumes from the coal tar factory, or brackish smells from the nearby river.The town’s air was consistently __________: depending on the breeze, one might be greeted with the sour effluvia of twenty breweries, choking fumes from the coal tar factory, or brackish smells from the nearby river.
答案：A, E　malodorous; noisome

#508 SE

答案：C, E　an anomaly; an aberration

#509 SE
Every illness is a story, and when Annie’s began it was characterized by the kinds of __________ details that mean nothing until seen in hindsight.Every illness is a story, and when Annie’s began it was characterized by the kinds of __________ details that mean nothing until seen in hindsight.
答案：B, F　unexceptional; nondescript

#510 SE
Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.
答案：B, F　collective; social
